seo.title: Building Code Generation with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programmatic LLM optimization for code generation agents that iteratively refine code through sandbox execution and testing. Guardrails are essential to prevent syntax errors, unsafe code execution, credential leaks, and overfitting during optimization. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
A typical DSPy code generation agent uses Signature modules for task decomposition: CodeWriter generates initial code, TestExecutor runs it in a sandbox, and Refiner iterates based on test failures. Modules are composed into a Program optimized via teleprompters like BootstrapFewShot. The forward() pass orchestrates the full pipeline, making it ideal for Vex wrapping.

The agent maintains state across iterations, tracking test results and refinement history. Sandbox execution uses restricted environments (Docker, E2E) to validate functionality while DSPy optimizes for higher pass@k rates. Output distributions can drift during composition, requiring verification at each module boundary.

Verification focuses on code safety (no syscalls, imports), syntax validity, dependency constraints, and no credential patterns before sandbox execution.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: Optimized CodeWriter generates dangerous syscalls or file writes during refinement
risks.mitigation: Vex safety scanner blocks execution before sandbox

risks.name: Syntax errors in refined code
risks.description: Refiner module produces syntactically invalid Python despite test optimization
risks.mitigation: Vex syntax validator rejects before execution

risks.name: Credential leakage
risks.description: Training examples with API keys cause overfitting to credential patterns
risks.mitigation: Vex PII scanner strips secrets from generated code

risks.name: Unsafe dependency imports
risks.description: Code imports subprocess/os despite dependency safety constraints

implementation.title: DSPy Code Generation Signature
implementation.description: Basic DSPy signature for code generation with test feedback
implementation.code:
import dspy

class CodeWriter(dspy.Signature):
    '''Generate Python code for a task.'''
    task_description = dspy.InputField()
    test_cases = dspy.InputField(desc='JSON test cases')
    code = dspy.OutputField(desc='Valid Python code')

class CodeTester(dspy.Signature):
    '''Test generated code.'''
    code = dspy.InputField()
    test_cases = dspy.InputField()
    test_results = dspy.OutputField(desc='Pass/fail JSON')

codegen = dspy.ChainOfThought(CodeWriter)
test_module = dspy.ChainOfThought(CodeTester)

implementation.title: Composable DSPy Program
implementation.description: Full agent program with iterative refinement

implementation.title: Bootstrap Optimization
implementation.description: Optimize the full program with few-shot examples
implementation.code:
from dspy.teleprompt import BootstrapFewShot

program = CodeGenProgram()
trainset = [
    dspy.Example(task='sort list', test_cases='[[3,1,2]] -> [1,2,3]').with_inputs('task', 'test_cases')
]

teleprompter = BootstrapFewShot(metric=lambda ex, pred, trace: 'PASS' in pred.test_results)
optimized_program = teleprompter.compile(program, trainset=trainset)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Program forward() with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Verify entire DSPy program execution with comprehensive checks

vexIntegration.title: Module-Level Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify individual DSPy modules before composition

vexIntegration.title: Optimization-Time Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify during DSPy teleprompter compilation to prevent bad examples
vexIntegration.code:
from dspy.teleprompt import BootstrapFewShot
import vex

@vex.verify([vex.safety.Sandbox(), vex.pii.Credentials()])
def verified_example_gen(example):
    '''Generate safe training examples for optimization'''
    result = program(**example.inputs())
    if result.is_verified:
        return result
    raise ValueError(f'Unsafe example blocked: {result.violations}')

# Safe optimization
teleprompter = BootstrapFewShot(metric=verified_metric)
optimized = teleprompter.compile(program, trainset=trainset.map(verified_example_gen))

cta.heading: Secure Your DSPy Code Generation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your DSPy modules to eliminate code generation risks.
